steps.title: Line up like terms
steps.content: Subtracting a polynomial changes the sign of every term being subtracted, then matching powers of $x$ can be combined.

steps.title: Combine each power of x
steps.content: The cubic terms give $$9x^3+6x^3=15x^3.$$ The quadratic term is $5x^2$, the linear term is $5x$, and the constants give $$7-5=2.$$ So the expression is $$15x^3+5x^2+5x+2.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That expression matches Choice D — $15{x}^{3}+5{x}^{2}+5x+2$, so D is correct.
